question: What is the origin of COVID-19 — zoonotic spillover or research-related lab leak?
shape: contested debate with multiple analysts reaching opposite conclusions from shared evidence

sources.title: Practically-A-Book Review: Rootclaim $100,000 Lab Leak Debate
sources.author: Scott Alexander
sources.role: core case material — the central synthesis of the debate; leans zoonosis

sources.title: A Bayesian analysis of the COVID origins debate
sources.author: Eric Stansifer (debate judge; applied mathematician, PhD MIT)
sources.role: a debate judge's own written Bayesian reasoning toward zoonosis (his decision itself is video/PDF-only)

sources.title: Response to Michael Weissman re. covid origins debate
sources.author: Eric Stansifer (debate judge)
sources.role: direct rebuttal of Weissman's lab-leak Bayesian analysis — a genuine cross-source contradiction

sources.title: An Inconvenient Probability (Bayesian analysis of COVID origins)
sources.author: Michael Weissman (physicist, UIUC)
sources.role: rigorous independent Bayesian analysis reaching ~300:1 for lab leak — the opposite pole to Stansifer

sources.title: COVID origins debate: Response to Scott Alexander
sources.author: Saar Wilf / Rootclaim
sources.role: the losing side's rebuttal — key for representing disagreement faithfully

known_crux_hint: Multiple sources indicate the zoonosis conclusion leans heavily on one claim: that the early case cluster at the Huanan Seafood Market is strong evidence for zoonosis. Removing/weakening it reportedly flips the overall conclusion. A successful crux-detection layer should surface this automatically from the multi-source graph — NOT be hand-coded to find it.
note: Genuinely multi-source and two-sided: 3 zoonosis (Scott Alexander synthesis + judge Eric Stansifer's Bayesian analysis and his rebuttal of Weissman) vs 3 lab_leak (Weissman's ~300:1 Bayesian + Rootclaim's response and results). Two opposing formal Bayesian analyses (Weissman vs Stansifer) instantiate the brief's 'independent analyses spanning orders of magnitude'. Judge Will Van Treuren's decision is video-only and not cleanly ingestible as text; Judge Eric's written reasoning stands in for the judge voice. Raw texts fetched not redistributed.
